name: William S. Burroughs

acts.description: Shot and killed his wife Joan Vollmer in a drunken William Tell stunt in Mexico City, 1951

behavior_summary: Shot and killed his common-law wife Joan Vollmer at a party in Mexico City while attempting a drunken William Tell act. Fled to avoid charges. Later said the act made him a writer.

art.summary: Central figure of the Beat Generation. His cut-up technique influenced generations of writers, musicians, and artists. Naked Lunch remains a landmark of American avant-garde literature.

accountability_events: 1952 fled Mexico, charged in absentia with homicide

verdict.blurb: He shot his wife in the head and credited her death with making him a writer. She was 28. He got away with it, moved to Tangier, and wrote some of the most influential prose of the twentieth century. The dead woman's name was Joan.
